seo.title: Krishan Mohan — Hire AI-Native Next.js & React Engineer | 9+ yrs, India
seo.description: Hire Krishan Mohan — a senior AI-native software engineer (9+ years, ex-McKinsey) shipping React, Next.js and LLM-powered products 3–5× faster with spec-driven development. Available for contracts & fractional CTO work worldwide.
seo.ogAlt: Krishan Mohan — AI-Native Software Engineer. 9+ years. Ships with spec-driven AI workflows.

person.name: Krishan Mohan
person.tagline: Senior software engineer shipping AI-native products with spec-driven development.

faqs.q: What services does Krishan Mohan offer?
faqs.a: Full-stack Next.js and React development, LLM and RAG integration, micro-frontend architecture, Core Web Vitals performance audits, team onboarding to spec-driven development, and fractional AI-native CTO engagements.

faqs.q: How long has Krishan been building web products?
faqs.a: 9+ years of production experience across retail, fintech, SaaS, and media — currently Frontend Architect at Alshaya Group, previously at McKinsey & Company, Arts Consolidated (Operabase, CueTV), and IDFC Bank.

Spec-driven development is a workflow where you write a precise, reviewable specification first, then let AI agents implement and test against it. It produces 3–5× faster delivery with higher test coverage than traditional prompt-by-prompt coding.
Yes — fully remote engagements worldwide across time zones. Short-term sprints, multi-month builds, and fractional CTO retainers are all available.

faqs.q: How fast can a project start?
faqs.a: Projects typically kick off within a week of the first call. Most engagements begin with a paid 1–2 week discovery & spec phase before full build.

faqs.q: What AI stack does Krishan specialize in?
faqs.a: OpenAI GPT-4/4o, Anthropic Claude, LangChain & LangGraph agents, pgvector / Pinecone / Weaviate for RAG, Cursor AI for spec-driven engineering, and Vercel AI SDK for production deployments.

boot.title: SESSION_001.boot
boot.tagline: This is the story of an engineer who stopped typing every character — and started directing an AI fleet.

chapters.label: GENESIS
chapters.title: It started with a curly brace.
paragraphs: New Delhi. A cold morning. My first checked-in commit at ProntoItlab. The code was ugly. The deploy was scary. I loved every second of it.
Back then, shipping anything meant typing every character by hand. Stack Overflow in one tab, docs in the other. A feature was three days of work — one day to think, two to debug.

chapters.label: CRAFT
chapters.title: Six years of shipping, mostly manual.
paragraphs: Keurig checkout flow from Hybris → React. Flyrobe end-to-end, ₹5M funded on Shark Tank. Preqin dashboards for hedge funds. IDFC Bank's DataHive in an Nx mono-repo.
I learned that code isn't the bottleneck — context is. I started obsessing over architecture: micro-frontends, SSR, composable component systems. The craft was satisfying. But it was slow.

chapters.label: SIGNALS
chapters.title: The first strange signal.
paragraphs: ChatGPT dropped. Copilot became useful. At first I dismissed it — a toy. A stochastic parrot. I wrote snark in Slack and kept shipping the old way.
Then a junior dev on my team wrote a feature in 4 hours that would have taken me 2 days. Same quality. I stopped laughing.
Something was happening. I didn't fully understand it yet — but I had to find out.

chapters.label: AWAKENING
chapters.title: I stopped writing code. I started directing it.
paragraphs: Cursor. Claude. GPT-4. Devin. I went deep. Not just as tools — as a new interface for engineering itself.
I rewrote my daily loop. Instead of typing, I described. Instead of debugging line-by-line, I gave the model the failing test. Instead of reading docs, I asked. The velocity went from 1x to 3x to 5x.
And something deeper changed: my attention moved up the stack. Away from syntax. Toward intent, contracts, constraints, and taste.

artifact.code:
# 2016 mindset
write every character.
compile mental model of the codebase.
debug in your head.

# 2024 mindset
write the spec.
direct the agent.
review the diff.
ship.

chapters.label: METHOD
chapters.title: Spec-Driven Development.
paragraphs: Here is the loop I live in every day. Write a spec — precise, typed, edge-cases spelled out. Hand it to the AI with context. Review the generated code ruthlessly. Test, iterate, ship.
The engineer's superpower is no longer typing speed. It is taste, context, and clarity of intent. The AI handles the rest.

chapters.label: FLEET
chapters.title: The AI stack I run.
paragraphs: I don't have favorites anymore. I route tasks to the model that fits. Coding in Cursor, reasoning with Claude, quick drafts with GPT, autonomy via Devin, chains through LangChain, embeddings through OpenAI APIs.
Each is a specialist. My job is to orchestrate them.

chapters.label: NOW
chapters.title: Alshaya. Micro-frontends. AI co-pilots.
paragraphs: Frontend Architect at Alshaya Group, Gurugram. I own frontend architecture across a multi-brand retail ecosystem — micro-frontends, design-system governance, CI/CD, code reviews, and an engineering culture built around AI-augmented delivery.
But the way I work today has very little in common with 2016. I write specs. The AI writes the code. Together, we ship faster than any team I've ever been on.

specWorkflow.heading: The Spec-Driven Loop
specWorkflow.description: Every feature I ship moves through this five-step loop. The AI does the grunt work. I do the thinking.

steps.name: SPEC
steps.desc: Write the intent. Types, contracts, edge cases, acceptance criteria. If a human can't read it, an AI can't build it.

steps.name: PROMPT
steps.desc: Feed the spec to the model with context — repo, conventions, prior art, constraints.

steps.name: GENERATE
steps.desc: The AI drafts the implementation, tests, and docs. Often in parallel, across multiple files.

steps.name: VERIFY
steps.desc: Read every diff. Run the tests. Refactor for taste. Loop until it's correct and clean.

steps.name: SHIP
steps.desc: Merge. Deploy. Observe. Feed learnings back into the next spec.

services.title: AI-Augmented Product Engineering
services.desc: Full-stack Next.js / React apps built with a spec-driven AI loop. 3–5x faster delivery with higher test coverage.

services.title: Team Onboarding to Spec-Driven Dev
services.desc: Workshops, playbooks, and hands-on coaching to transform your team's AI workflow from messy prompting to disciplined shipping.

services.title: Fractional AI-Native CTO
services.desc: Architecture reviews, hiring support, roadmap, and technical due diligence. Especially for AI-first startups.
